title: senna chapmanii
intro: Senna chapmanii, also known as the desert senna, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is a shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, known for its medicinal properties, particularly as a laxative. The plant contains anthraquinone glycosides, which have been used to treat constipation and promote bowel movements, making it a natural remedy for digestive issues. The senna leaves and pods can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried extract, and are also used in herbal supplements to promote regular bowel movements and relieve symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
